title: carex humilis var. scirrobasis
intro: Carex humilis var. scirrobasis, also known as the small sedge, is a type of plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae) and is native to North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Carex humilis var. scirrobasis include its potential to aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or alcohol to create teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use.
